project.name: dotfiles
project.description: A personal macOS dotfiles repository managed with Chezmoi and age-encrypted secrets, which also vendors three small Python uv tools (mcp_sync, aws_config_gen, token_auditor) that ship alongside the configuration.

nodes.name: .aiignore
nodes.summary: Gitignore-syntax exclusion list telling AI tooling to skip transient artifacts like .DS_Store, logs, and build/dist/out directories.

nodes.name: .chezmoi.toml.tmpl
nodes.summary: Chezmoi bootstrap config template that prompts once per machine for machine-type and email, then writes the cached chezmoi.toml with age encryption identity and the .machine/.email data variables that drive all downstream gating.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses chezmoi-init-only template functions (promptStringOnce) that are unavailable outside the init context, so this file must be excluded from bulk execute-template walks.

nodes.name: machines.toml
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for machine-type capability gating: maps each machine (personal-mac, work-mac, lab-mac) to capability booleans (tiling, atuin, mcp, skills, gui, dev, aws_sso, infra) that templates and .chezmoiignore gate on.
nodes.languageNotes: Capability-based gating lets adding a new machine be a one-row change instead of editing every gate site; templates access it via (index .machines .machine).<capability>.

nodes.name: .chezmoiencrypt.toml
nodes.summary: Declares the age encryption cipher/recipient and the glob patterns (zsh env files, .envrc, secret/password/token files) that chezmoi must encrypt at rest.

nodes.name: .chezmoiexternal.toml
nodes.summary: Declares the tmux plugin manager (tpm) as an external git-repo dependency that chezmoi clones into .config/tmux/plugins and refreshes weekly.

nodes.name: .chezmoiignore
nodes.summary: Templated deployment manifest that gates which source files chezmoi deploys per machine, combining static excludes (in-repo Python tools, transient .claude data) with capability- and prefix-based conditionals reading from machines.toml.
nodes.languageNotes: Itself a Go template: {{ if not (index .machines .machine).<cap> }} blocks skip whole config subtrees on machines lacking a capability; also the single kill-switch for the xcode-mcp-proxy LaunchAgent.

nodes.name: run_once_setup-macos.sh
nodes.summary: Run-once post-apply hook for first-time macOS setup: runs brew bundle/doctor health checks against the global Brewfile and installs rustup if absent.
nodes.languageNotes: chezmoi run_once_ prefix means it executes only on the first apply per machine.

nodes.name: run_onchange_after_load-xcode-mcp-proxy.sh.tmpl
nodes.summary: Personal-macOS-only run-onchange hook that installs mcp-proxy and (re)loads the xcode-mcp-proxy LaunchAgent via launchctl, so MCP clients share one supervised mcpbridge process behind a local HTTP endpoint.
nodes.languageNotes: run_onchange_ re-runs when the embedded plist sha256sum changes; gated to (personal-mac && darwin), with the .chezmoiignore line as the real kill-switch.

nodes.name: run_onchange_configure-macos-defaults.sh
nodes.summary: Run-onchange hook asserting intentional macOS preferences via the `defaults` command (dark mode, key repeat, dock, Finder, screenshots, trackpad, Activity Monitor), then restarts Dock/Finder/SystemUIServer to apply them.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses only Apple-supported defaults keys so SOC-locked machines won't flag it; re-runs whenever the file hash changes to re-assert values.

nodes.name: run_after_sync-aws-config.sh.tmpl
nodes.summary: Post-apply hook that runs the vendored aws_config_gen tool to regenerate AWS SSO profiles, self-gating on the aws_sso machine capability so it no-ops on machines without AWS access.
nodes.languageNotes: Go-templated shell: the {{ if not (index .machines .machine).aws_sso }} guard collapses the body to exit 0 on non-AWS machines; honors AWS_CONFIG_GEN_STRICT for fail-vs-warn behavior.

nodes.name: run_after_sync-mcp.sh.tmpl
nodes.summary: Post-apply hook that invokes the vendored mcp_sync tool (sync-mcp-configs) to fan out per-tool MCP configs, selecting the machine overlay from chezmoi's .machine data and self-gating on the mcp capability.
nodes.languageNotes: Renders the correct machine overlay (personal/work/lab) at apply time rather than trusting on-disk sort order; MCP_SYNC_STRICT toggles fail-or-warn.

nodes.name: run_after_sync-skills.sh.tmpl
nodes.summary: Post-apply hook that runs the sync-skills entry point of the mcp_sync project to populate ~/.claude/skills/ from vendored and personal skills, self-gating on the skills machine capability.
nodes.languageNotes: sync-skills ships as a second entry point of the mcp_sync uv project; selects the per-machine skills overlay the same way the MCP hook does.

nodes.name: commit.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code slash-command prompt that instructs the agent to stage all uncommitted changes and create an atomic Conventional-Commit-tagged commit.

nodes.name: create-prd.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code slash-command prompt defining a full PRD template (required/optional sections, instructions, style guidelines) for generating a Product Requirements Document from conversation context.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill definition for verifying chezmoi state before apply: render every *.tmpl via execute-template to catch parse errors that diff hides, then preview the diff and optionally dry-run, keyed off the active machine's data.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill definition for static analysis of the capability gating system, driving audit.sh to detect orphan capabilities, undefined gates, and hasPrefix gates that should migrate to the machines.toml capability table.

nodes.name: audit.sh
nodes.summary: Static-analysis script that parses machines.toml for defined capabilities, greps every .tmpl and .chezmoiignore for (index .machines .machine).<cap> consumers, and reports orphans, undefined gates, and prefix-gate migration candidates, exiting non-zero on actionable findings.
nodes.languageNotes: Procedural bash with awk extraction and associative-array hit maps; roots itself via chezmoi source-path or REPO_ROOT override and is CI/pre-commit ready via its non-zero exit.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill definition for verifying the MCP sync pipeline: lint and test mcp_sync, list deployment targets via list_targets.py, then sandbox-diff generated tool configs against deployed copies using dry_run_diff.sh.

nodes.name: dry_run_diff.sh
nodes.summary: Runs mcp_sync against a throwaway sandbox HOME (since mcp_sync has no --dry-run flag), seeding it with the master config and overrides, then diffs every generated file against the deployed copy to surface drift non-destructively.
nodes.languageNotes: Discovers deployment targets dynamically by importing mcp_sync.sync._build_targets in an inline Python heredoc, so the diff list never goes stale when sync.py adds a target.

nodes.name: list_targets.py
nodes.summary: Helper that prints the full set of paths mcp_sync will overwrite, discovered dynamically from sync._build_targets plus the three special-cased writers (codex, claude.json patch, copilot-cli) so it never goes stale.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Builds the mcp_sync deployment-target list from _build_targets(Path.home()) and prints each destination (and legacy destination) plus the three special-cased writer paths.

nodes.name: aws-config-gen-ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that lints (ruff) and tests (pytest with coverage) the aws_config_gen tool on pushes and PRs touching aws_config_gen/**.

nodes.name: dotfiles-hygiene-ci.yml
nodes.summary: Repo-wide hygiene workflow that runs pre-commit on all files, validates the MCP master config structure with inline Python, and bash -n syntax-checks every shell script under .chezmoiscripts and scripts.

nodes.name: mcp-sync-ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that lints and tests the mcp_sync tool (and smoke-tests sync-skills --help) on changes to mcp_sync/**, the skills sync hook, or machines.toml.

nodes.name: token-auditor-ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that syncs deps, lints (ruff), type-checks (ty), smoke-tests the CLIs, and runs the full pytest suite for the token_auditor tool with uv caching.

nodes.name: .gitleaks.toml
nodes.summary: Gitleaks secret-scanning config that extends the default ruleset and allowlists age-encrypted (encrypted_*.age) files so encrypted-at-rest blobs don't trip the scanner.

nodes.name: .pre-commit-config.yaml
nodes.summary: Pre-commit hook configuration wiring repo-wide hygiene checks (formatting, ruff, test-name conventions) that run on commit and push.

nodes.name: mcp-sync-all.run.xml
nodes.summary: IntelliJ/PyCharm run configuration that executes the full mcp_sync pytest suite from the project tests directory.

nodes.name: token-auditor-all.run.xml
nodes.summary: IntelliJ/PyCharm run configuration that executes the full token_auditor pytest suite from the project tests directory.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Codex agent guidance (symlink twin of CLAUDE.md) documenting repo purpose, per-tool commands, chezmoi naming conventions, machine-capability gating, and commit conventions across 18 sections.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Primary Claude Code project guidance covering the dotfiles layout, the three vendored Python uv tools, the MCP sync system, and the machine-capability gating model in 18 sections.

nodes.name: Justfile
nodes.summary: Just task runner defining recipes for chezmoi apply/diff plus lint/test/format wrappers for the mcp_sync and aws_config_gen uv projects, aggregate lint/test/check targets, and a capability audit.
nodes.languageNotes: Just recipes are thin shell wrappers; aggregate recipes (lint, test, check) compose other recipes as dependencies.

nodes.name: com.user.xcode-mcp-proxy.plist.tmpl
nodes.summary: Chezmoi-templated macOS LaunchAgent that keeps an mcp-proxy daemon alive on 127.0.0.1:9876 bridging to xcrun mcpbridge, with home-dir paths injected via Go template variables.
nodes.languageNotes: launchd plist with RunAtLoad+KeepAlive for a persistent background agent; chezmoi {{ .chezmoi.homeDir }} substitution makes it portable across machines.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Top-level repository documentation with fresh-OS bootstrap steps, age encryption key setup, common chezmoi commands, MCP config sync workflow, and environment-variable management across 20 sections.

nodes.name: .python-version
nodes.summary: Pins the Python interpreter version used by uv for the aws_config_gen tool.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive guide for the aws_config_gen tool covering installation, CLI usage, the overrides.json generator config, the SSO discovery pipeline, profile-naming logic, config-file merge behavior, and troubleshooting across 27 sections.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Packaging and tooling config for the aws_config_gen uv project, declaring the aws-config-gen console script, the dev dependency group, pytest options, and setuptools package discovery.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker establishing aws_config_gen as an importable Python package.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: Module entrypoint enabling `python -m aws_config_gen`, delegating to and exiting with the cli() return code.

nodes.name: cli.py
nodes.summary: Command-line orchestrator that parses args, loads the generator config, runs SSO role discovery, builds profile entries, and renders/writes the AWS config, handling token and network errors with strict/non-strict exit semantics.

nodes.name: config_writer.py
nodes.summary: Renders profile entries into AWS config stanzas and atomically merges the generated block into an existing ~/.aws/config between managed markers, preserving manual sections and file permissions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an atomic write (tmp file + os.replace) and managed BEGIN/END markers to merge generated content without clobbering hand-written profiles.

nodes.name: discovery.py
nodes.summary: Discovery pipeline that loads the SSO token then enumerates every account and its assignable roles, applying a skip list, into a flat list of AccountRole objects.

nodes.name: naming.py
nodes.summary: Loads the overrides.json generator config and derives unique, human-friendly AWS profile names from discovered account/role pairs, validating against name collisions.

nodes.name: sso_client.py
nodes.summary: Thin AWS SSO portal HTTP client using urllib to list accounts and per-account roles, paginating results into typed SSOAccount records.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses stdlib urllib.request rather than boto3, keeping the tool dependency-free per repo policy.

nodes.name: sso_token.py
nodes.summary: Loads a cached AWS SSO access token from the sha1-keyed cache file, raising TokenNotFoundError or TokenExpiredError when the token is missing or past its expiry.

nodes.name: types.py
nodes.summary: Defines the frozen dataclasses that form the tool's data model: SSOAccount, AccountRole, ProfileEntry, and the GeneratorConfig overrides shape.
nodes.languageNotes: Frozen dataclasses give immutable, hashable value objects passed through the discovery -> naming -> writer pipeline.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker making the aws_config_gen tests directory an importable package.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Shared pytest fixtures providing a sample GeneratorConfig and a set of sample SSOAccount records for the aws_config_gen test suite.

nodes.name: test_cli.py
nodes.summary: End-to-end CLI tests covering dry-run output, strict vs non-strict exit codes on token/network errors, malformed and missing generator configs, duplicate-profile detection, and HTTP 401 login messaging.

nodes.name: test_config_writer.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for profile rendering and the managed-block config merge, asserting marker handling, manual-section preservation, atomic writes, file-mode preservation, and corrupted-marker error cases.

nodes.name: test_discovery.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the role discovery pipeline verifying full enumeration, skip-list filtering, empty-account handling, and that the cache_dir is threaded through to load_sso_token.

nodes.name: build_parser
nodes.summary: Constructs the argparse parser defining the --config, --generator-config, --dry-run, and --strict command-line options.

nodes.name: cli
nodes.summary: Main CLI flow: loads the generator config, discovers SSO roles, builds profile entries, then renders and writes the AWS config, returning exit codes per strict/non-strict error policy.

nodes.name: render_profiles
nodes.summary: Renders a list of ProfileEntry objects into the text of an AWS config managed block, including sso-session and per-profile stanzas.

nodes.name: merge_config
nodes.summary: Merges a generated block into existing config content between BEGIN/END markers, validating marker integrity, absorbing duplicate sections, and preserving surrounding manual content.

nodes.name: write_config
nodes.summary: Atomically writes the merged AWS config to disk via a temp file and os.replace, preserving the existing file mode or defaulting to owner-only permissions.

nodes.name: _remove_sections
nodes.summary: Strips the named profile/sso-session sections out of config content, collapsing the resulting extra blank lines.

nodes.name: discover_all_roles
nodes.summary: Loads the SSO token then walks every account and role from the SSO client, filtering skipped account/role pairs, and returns a flat AccountRole list.

nodes.name: load_generator_config
nodes.summary: Parses the overrides.json file into a GeneratorConfig, including account-name and role-short-name maps and the parsed skip list.

nodes.name: _validate_unique_profile_names
nodes.summary: Raises a ValueError listing any profile names that collide after generation, ensuring every emitted AWS profile is uniquely addressable.

nodes.name: build_profile_entries
nodes.summary: Transforms discovered AccountRole pairs into ProfileEntry objects, applying account-name and role-short-name overrides and validating uniqueness before returning a sorted list.

nodes.name: list_accounts
nodes.summary: Calls the AWS SSO portal list-accounts endpoint with pagination and returns the accounts as typed SSOAccount records.

nodes.name: list_account_roles
nodes.summary: Calls the AWS SSO portal list-account-roles endpoint for a given account id, paginating to collect every assignable role name.

nodes.name: load_sso_token
nodes.summary: Resolves and reads the sha1-keyed AWS SSO token cache file, raising TokenNotFoundError when absent and TokenExpiredError when the cached token has expired.

nodes.name: TokenExpiredError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when the cached AWS SSO token exists but is past its expiry timestamp.

nodes.name: TokenNotFoundError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when no cached AWS SSO token file can be found for the requested session.

nodes.name: SSOAccount
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding an AWS account's id, name, and email address as returned by the SSO portal.

nodes.name: AccountRole
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass pairing an SSOAccount with a single assignable role name, the unit emitted by discovery.

nodes.name: ProfileEntry
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass describing a fully-resolved AWS profile (name, sso-session, account id, role, region) ready to be rendered into config.

nodes.name: GeneratorConfig
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass capturing the overrides.json generator settings: account-name and role-short-name maps, skip list, default region, and SSO session details.

nodes.name: sample_generator_config
nodes.summary: Pytest fixture returning a representative GeneratorConfig with sample account-name and role-short-name overrides for use across tests.

nodes.name: sample_accounts
nodes.summary: Pytest fixture returning a set of sample SSOAccount records used to drive discovery and naming tests.

nodes.name: TestMergeConfigCorruptedMarkers
nodes.summary: Test class asserting merge_config raises on corrupted managed markers: duplicate begins, end-before-begin, duplicate ends, and a begin without an end.

nodes.name: TestDiscoverAllRoles
nodes.summary: Test class covering discover_all_roles: full enumeration, skip-list filtering of matching and non-matching pairs, empty accounts, and cache_dir propagation to load_sso_token.

nodes.name: test_integration.py
nodes.summary: End-to-end integration tests for the aws-config-gen CLI: drives the full pipeline against a fake SSO token cache and a mocked urlopen, asserting dry-run output, config writing, and marker-based merge that preserves manual edits.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses unittest.mock.patch over urllib.request.urlopen plus pytest tmp_path/capsys fixtures to exercise the whole CLI without real AWS calls.

nodes.name: _mock_urlopen
nodes.summary: Test double for urllib urlopen that returns canned account-list and role-list JSON payloads keyed off the request URL.

nodes.name: test_full_pipeline_dry_run
nodes.summary: Asserts a dry-run CLI invocation prints the would-be config and writes nothing to disk.

nodes.name: test_full_pipeline_writes_config
nodes.summary: Asserts the CLI generates and writes the expected AWS profile config to the target path.

nodes.name: test_marker_based_merge_preserves_manual_content
nodes.summary: Verifies the marker-delimited merge replaces only the managed block and leaves hand-written config above and below the markers intact.

nodes.name: test_naming.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for profile-name construction: account/role-name overrides and fallbacks, single-vs-multi-role suffix logic, alphabetical sorting, duplicate-name detection, and generator-config loading/validation.

nodes.name: test_duplicate_profile_names_raise_error
nodes.summary: Asserts build_profile_entries raises when two accounts resolve to the same profile name.

nodes.name: test_load_generator_config
nodes.summary: Round-trips a generator config JSON file through load_generator_config and checks the parsed result type and fields.

nodes.name: test_load_generator_config_rejects_bad_skip_entry
nodes.summary: Asserts a malformed skip entry in the generator config causes load_generator_config to raise.

nodes.name: test_sso_client.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the AWS SSO HTTP client covering list_accounts and list_account_roles: single-page, pagination, empty results, header propagation, and HTTP error handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Tests are grouped into TestListAccounts / TestListAccountRoles classes; urlopen is patched and MagicMock fakes the response context manager.

nodes.name: TestListAccounts
nodes.summary: Test suite for list_accounts covering single page, pagination, empty list, and HTTP error paths.

nodes.name: TestListAccountRoles
nodes.summary: Test suite for list_account_roles covering single page, pagination, and HTTP error paths.

nodes.name: test_sso_token.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for load_sso_token: valid token retrieval, expiry rejection, missing/malformed cache files, missing required keys, and resolution of the default cache directory.
nodes.languageNotes: Reconstructs the SSO cache layout in tmp_path and hashes session names with sha1 to match the AWS CLI cache-file naming scheme.

nodes.name: _make_token
nodes.summary: Builds a token-cache JSON payload with a given expiry and access token for use across the token tests.

nodes.name: test_default_cache_dir_used
nodes.summary: Monkeypatches the default cache directory and asserts load_sso_token resolves tokens from it when no explicit dir is given.

nodes.name: dot_gitignore_global

nodes.name: copilot-instructions.md

nodes.name: Brewfile.tmpl

nodes.name: lab.json.tmpl

nodes.name: personal.json.tmpl

nodes.name: work.json
nodes.summary: Work-mac MCP overlay adding the read-only AWS Labs CCAPI MCP server (driven by AWS_PROFILE) on top of the shared master config.

nodes.name: mcp-master.json
nodes.summary: Base MCP master config shared across all machines; currently declares no servers, serving as the merge foundation that per-machine overlays extend.

nodes.name: config.toml.tmpl

nodes.languageNotes: Uses chezmoi Go-template machine-capability gate `{{ if (index .machines .machine).infra }}` to conditionally include infra tools per machine type.

nodes.name: config.nu

nodes.summary: Neoconf/neodev project settings enabling the Lua language server's Neovim runtime library and plugin completion for editing this Lua config.

nodes.summary: Neovim entry point that sets the space leader keys early and bootstraps the plugin manager by requiring the config.lazy module.

nodes.name: lazy-lock.json
nodes.summary: lazy.nvim plugin lockfile pinning the exact git commit of all 77 installed Neovim plugins (LazyVim, treesitter, LSP, completion, colorschemes, dev tooling) for reproducible installs.

nodes.name: lazyvim.json
nodes.summary: LazyVim metadata file recording enabled extras (language packs, DAP, linting, formatting) plus the installed LazyVim version and dismissed news entries.

nodes.name: autocmds.lua
nodes.summary: LazyVim autocommand customization hook, currently a documented placeholder where additional autocmds loaded on the VeryLazy event would be defined.

nodes.name: keymaps.lua
nodes.summary: Custom Neovim keymaps that rebind d and x in normal and visual modes to delete into the black-hole register so deletions never clobber the yank register.

nodes.name: lazy.lua
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the lazy.nvim plugin manager (cloning it on first run), then configures LazyVim with explicit early-loaded mason plugins, TypeScript/JSON extras, the local plugins directory, and update checking.
nodes.languageNotes: Imports the sibling `plugins` directory via lazy.nvim's `{ import = "plugins" }` spec, which auto-loads every Lua file under lua/plugins/.

nodes.name: options.lua
nodes.summary: LazyVim options customization hook, currently a documented placeholder where editor options loaded before lazy.nvim startup would be set.

nodes.name: mappings.lua
nodes.summary: Defensive shim ensuring `require('mason-lspconfig.mappings')` never errors during startup by loading the real mappings from the lazy install dir, or falling back to a minimal stub table when unavailable.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pcall/loadfile guards and package.loaded inspection to safely probe the upstream module without crashing on missing files.

nodes.name: try_load_real
nodes.summary: Attempts to locate and execute the real mason-lspconfig mappings file from the lazy data directory, returning its table or nil on any failure.

nodes.name: get_mason_map
nodes.summary: Fallback accessor returning the empty lspconfig-to-package and package-to-lspconfig maps, matching the upstream API shape so callers degrade gracefully.

nodes.name: example.lua
nodes.summary: lazy.nvim plugin spec configuring the everforest colorscheme, trouble.nvim diagnostics keymaps, nvim-cmp emoji source, telescope layout overrides, an extended treesitter parser list, and the amp.nvim AI assistant.
nodes.languageNotes: Returns a lazy.nvim spec table; each entry is a declarative plugin definition with opts/keys/config rather than imperative code.

nodes.name: lsp.lua
nodes.summary: lazy.nvim plugin spec wiring mason, mason-lspconfig, and nvim-lspconfig together to auto-install and configure language servers for Python, TypeScript, Lua, Terraform, Helm, Docker, and YAML (with SchemaStore and Kubernetes schemas).
nodes.languageNotes: Declarative LazyVim opts table; the YAML server is configured with schemaStore and explicit Kubernetes/docker-compose schema globs.

nodes.name: vim-be-better.lua
nodes.summary: lazy.nvim plugin spec installing the vim-be-better practice game plugin with debug logging enabled.

nodes.name: mise.toml

nodes.name: stylua.toml
nodes.summary: StyLua formatter configuration for the Neovim Lua config, setting 2-space indentation and a 120-column width.

nodes.name: rules.toml
nodes.summary: OpenCode safe-mode permission policy for unattended runs, restricting filesystem access, denying destructive shell/git commands, allowlisting network hosts, and capping process timeouts and resource usage.
nodes.languageNotes: Layered allow/deny/ask rule sections (filesystem, shell, network, git, process) define a least-privilege sandbox for overnight autonomous agent runs.

nodes.name: executable_sketchybarrc
nodes.summary: Main SketchyBar entry-point script that defines the Everforest Dark Hard color palette, configures the top bar and item defaults, registers the aerospace workspace-change event, and sources all bar item scripts.
nodes.languageNotes: Sources item scripts via `source "$ITEM_DIR/*.sh"`; sourcing order matters since the first right-side item sourced renders rightmost.

nodes.name: executable_battery.sh
nodes.summary: SketchyBar item registration for a battery indicator that subscribes to power-source-change and system-woke events and delegates rendering to the battery plugin script.

nodes.name: executable_clock.sh
nodes.summary: SketchyBar item registration defining a 24-hour clock and a US-date calendar with a hover popup showing UTC date and time, all driven by the clock plugin script.

nodes.name: executable_front_app.sh
nodes.summary: SketchyBar item registration for a center label showing the currently focused application, updated via the front_app_switched event and the front_app plugin script.

nodes.name: executable_volume.sh
nodes.summary: SketchyBar item registration for a volume mute indicator that subscribes to volume-change events and delegates rendering to the volume plugin script.

nodes.name: executable_wifi.sh
nodes.summary: SketchyBar item registration for an icon-only WiFi indicator that subscribes to wifi-change and system-woke events and delegates rendering to the wifi plugin script.

nodes.name: executable_workspaces.sh
nodes.summary: Defines the SketchyBar workspace indicator items: nine bracketed workspace groups each with pre-allocated app-icon slots, plus a single hidden controller item that subscribes to aerospace events and delegates all per-event redraw work to aerospace.sh.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a controller pattern (one hidden item subscribed to events) to avoid a 9x subprocess fanout that would occur if each workspace item had its own script= and --subscribe.

nodes.name: executable_aerospace.sh
nodes.summary: AeroSpace workspace controller plugin for SketchyBar that fires once per workspace/front-app event, queries aerospace a single time, and emits one batched sketchybar invocation updating every workspace's highlight, bracket color, and brand-colored app-icon slots.
nodes.languageNotes: Requires bash >= 4 for associative arrays (declare -A); resolves to brew bash via PATH. Notes a set -euo pipefail gotcha where ((i++)) exits non-zero on a zero pre-increment, working around it with arithmetic assignment.

nodes.name: color_for_app
nodes.summary: Maps a (lowercased) application name to its brand-specific icon color via a large case statement, falling back to a default gray for unknown apps.

nodes.name: executable_battery.sh
nodes.summary: SketchyBar battery plugin that parses pmset output for charge percentage, charging state, and time remaining, then sets a Material Design battery icon, color (green/yellow/red/aqua), and label.

nodes.name: executable_clock.sh
nodes.summary: SketchyBar clock/calendar plugin that updates the bar with local time and date, and lazily computes UTC labels for a hover popup only on mouse-enter to avoid per-tick subshells.

nodes.name: executable_front_app.sh
nodes.summary: Minimal SketchyBar plugin that updates an item's label to the focused application name from the front_app_switched event's INFO variable.

nodes.name: executable_icon_map.sh
nodes.summary: Vendored sketchybar-app-font icon map: a single large __icon_map function with a giant case statement translating application names (across many locales) into Nerd Font glyph tokens used by the workspace app-icon slots.
nodes.languageNotes: Generated lookup table (~1440 lines) where each app name maps to a :glyph: token; sourced by aerospace.sh which calls __icon_map per app.

nodes.name: __icon_map
nodes.summary: Sets icon_result to the Nerd Font glyph token matching the passed application name via an exhaustive case statement covering hundreds of apps and localized name variants.

nodes.name: executable_volume.sh
nodes.summary: SketchyBar volume plugin that queries macOS mute state via osascript and sets a muted or unmuted Material Design speaker icon with corresponding color.

nodes.name: executable_wifi.sh
nodes.summary: SketchyBar Wi-Fi plugin that asks macOS for the Wi-Fi service IP and shows a connected or disconnected Material Design icon, treating all not-connected states uniformly via an empty-IP check.

nodes.name: skills-master.json
nodes.summary: Master skills configuration listing skill sources (a pinned mattpocock git repo plus a local personal source) and the catalog of shared Claude Code skills deployed to all machines.

nodes.name: lab.json

nodes.name: personal.json
nodes.summary: Personal-machine skills overlay that adds local-source hippo skills (using-hippo-brain, monitoring-hippo) on top of the master catalog.

nodes.name: work.json
nodes.summary: Work-machine skills overlay that disables the general productivity/engineering skills from the master catalog, curating the skill set for the corporate machine.

nodes.name: dot_gitignore
nodes.summary: Gitignore for the tmux config directory that excludes TPM's tmp/ and plugins/ directories since the directory is symlinked and the plugin manager manages those paths.

nodes.name: executable_claude-pane-monitor.sh
nodes.summary: tmux status-bar helper that runs each status interval, forces all status backgrounds to match the Ghostty terminal background, and sets a per-window @claude_state (idle/working/empty) inferred from each pane's title so dotbar can stoplight-color Claude Code sessions.
nodes.languageNotes: Invoked from dotbar's status-right via #(); produces only side effects (tmux set-option), no stdout output for the status line itself.

nodes.name: tmux.conf
nodes.summary: Primary tmux configuration: C-Space prefix, vim-style pane navigation, an inline everforest dark-medium palette, dotbar status styling wired to a Claude-state monitor, TPM plugin list, and post-TPM overrides that use pane titles as window names.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines the everforest palette inline (no theme plugin) so the dotbar window-status formats can be fully controlled and conditionally colored by @claude_state.

nodes.name: config.toml
nodes.summary: Worktrunk (wt) git-worktree manager configuration setting the worktree path template, list defaults (CI/diffstat/LLM summaries), commit staging and Claude-haiku-backed commit-message generation, squash template, merge defaults, and the switch picker pager.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a Tera/Jinja-style template for the squash commit prompt and shells out to a headless `claude` invocation for LLM commit-message generation.

nodes.name: init.lua

nodes.name: readonly_LICENSE
nodes.summary: MIT License covering the vendored DuckDB yazi preview plugin.

nodes.name: readonly_README.md
nodes.summary: Vendored documentation for the duckdb.yazi previewer plugin, covering installation, yazi.toml/init.lua/keymap wiring, configuration options, and DuckDB-backed preview features across 20 sections.

nodes.name: readonly_main.lua
nodes.summary: DuckDB-powered yazi previewer plugin that renders CSV/TSV/Parquet/Excel/Avro/JSON and DuckDB databases as duckbox tables, with summarized/standard modes, lateral column scrolling, and a parquet preview cache.
nodes.languageNotes: Lua plugin using yazi's ya.sync state closures and Command spawning; builds SQL via string.format with extensive CASE-based numeric humanization in the summary CTE.

nodes.name: update_state
nodes.summary: ya.sync state accessor implementing set/get/check/clear actions over categorized subtables, the backbone for plugin options and cache bookkeeping.

nodes.name: duckdb_opener
nodes.summary: ya.sync opener that builds a DuckDB CLI invocation for the hovered file, loading spatial/avro extensions as needed and creating a table from the data source before launching the interactive UI.

nodes.name: generate_summary_cte
nodes.summary: Builds the SQL common table expression for SUMMARIZE output, humanizing numeric avg/std/quartile columns into k/m/b suffixes and formatting timestamp quartiles.

nodes.name: run_query
nodes.summary: Executes one or more DuckDB queries via the CLI with duckbox rendering config sized to the preview area, returning captured stdout/stderr output.

nodes.name: generate_db_query
nodes.summary: Generates the scroll-aware SQL to list a DuckDB database's tables, row/column counts, and column names, switching projection based on horizontal scroll position.

nodes.name: generate_standard_query
nodes.summary: Builds the standard-mode preview query, computing a visible column window from the preview width and scroll offset and optionally prepending a row-number column.

nodes.name: generate_summarized_query
nodes.summary: Assembles the summarized-mode preview query, selecting a scrollable subset of statistic columns wrapped around the summary CTE with limit/offset paging.

nodes.name: generate_peek_query
nodes.summary: Dispatches to the correct preview query generator based on mode, file type, and whether the target is the original file or a cache, with parquet-metadata fast paths and placeholder columns.

nodes.name: prepare_peek_context
nodes.summary: Resolves the peek rendering context: scroll reset handling, cache-path resolution and validity checks, target selection, and limit/offset computation for the preview area.

nodes.name: create_cache
nodes.summary: Materializes a parquet preview cache for a given mode by COPYing the preload query to disk, tracking preloading/bad-cache lists and cleaning up partial files on failure.

nodes.name: is_plain_text
nodes.summary: Detects single-column text files (which should fall back to the code previewer) by counting columns via DuckDB read_csv, caching the result per file.

nodes.name: M:preload
nodes.summary: Plugin preload hook that warms both standard and summarized parquet caches for supported file types, short-circuiting for plain-text and DuckDB database files.

nodes.name: M:peek
nodes.summary: Primary preview hook that runs the generated peek query, renders duckbox output, recovers from bad caches, and re-peeks once a fresh summarized cache completes.

nodes.name: M:entry
nodes.summary: Plugin command entry point that toggles preview mode, opens the DuckDB UI, or adjusts lateral column scroll based on the invoking argument.

nodes.name: readonly_LICENSE
nodes.summary: MIT license file vendored with the git.yazi plugin.

nodes.name: readonly_README.md
nodes.summary: Vendored documentation for the git.yazi plugin, describing installation, setup of the git status fetcher and linemode, and advanced theming options.

nodes.name: readonly_main.lua
nodes.summary: git.yazi plugin that shells out to `git status --porcelain` to compute per-file change codes, bubbling status up through directories and rendering linemode signs in the file list.
nodes.languageNotes: Lua plugin with LuaCATS type annotations; uses an UnstableFetcher contract plus ya.sync add/remove for shared repo state.

nodes.name: match
nodes.summary: Parses a single `git status --porcelain` line into a status code and path, handling quoted paths, Windows separators, and ignored-directory exclusion.

nodes.name: root
nodes.summary: Walks up the directory tree to locate the enclosing git repository root, treating both .git directories and worktree gitfiles as valid.

nodes.name: bubble_up
nodes.summary: Propagates the highest-priority change code from files up to their ancestor directories so folders reflect the status of their contents.

nodes.name: propagate_down
nodes.summary: Marks the current directory or its direct subdirectories as excluded/ignored based on git's ignored-directory list, scoped narrowly for performance.

nodes.name: setup
nodes.summary: Initializes plugin state and registers a Linemode children renderer that draws themed git status signs next to files in the current directory.

nodes.name: fetch
nodes.summary: UnstableFetcher that runs git status for the job's files, parses results into change/excluded sets, applies bubble-up/propagate-down, and commits the computed statuses to shared state.

nodes.name: readonly_types.lua
nodes.summary: LuaCATS type annotations for the git.yazi plugin defining the State, Options, Changes, and UnstableFetcher shapes.

nodes.name: readonly_LICENSE
nodes.summary: MIT license file vendored with the toggle-pane.yazi plugin.

nodes.name: readonly_README.md
nodes.summary: Vendored documentation for the toggle-pane.yazi plugin, explaining installation, the min/max pane subcommands, and keymap usage tips.

nodes.name: readonly_main.lua
nodes.summary: git.yazi-style layout plugin that overrides the tab layout to minimize or maximize the parent/current/preview panes via min-*/max-* subcommands, restoring the original on reset.
nodes.languageNotes: Sync-entry Lua plugin that monkey-patches Tab.layout with a custom ui.Layout ratio split and emits app:resize.

nodes.name: entry
nodes.summary: Sync entry point that toggles a target pane between zero/full ratio (min) or full-screen (max), swapping in a custom Tab.layout and restoring it when no action is given.

nodes.summary: Yazi theme selector setting the dark flavor to flexoki-dark.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for mcp_sync that re-exports the public API surface from the sync module.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: Module entry point enabling `python -m mcp_sync` by delegating to the CLI.

nodes.name: cli.py
nodes.summary: Argparse-based command-line interface for the MCP sync tool, parsing --home/--master/--machine-config paths and invoking run_sync.

nodes.name: build_parser
nodes.summary: Constructs the argparse ArgumentParser with the home, master, and machine-config path options for the sync CLI.

nodes.name: cli
nodes.summary: CLI entry function that parses argv, expands user paths, and calls run_sync with the resolved arguments.

nodes.name: skills.py
nodes.summary: Core implementation of the skill-sync subsystem: resolves a skills manifest, clones/refreshes git sources into a cache with TTL, deploys skills as symlinks or symlink-free copies, garbage-collects stale deployments, and persists sync state.
nodes.languageNotes: Defensive path-safety throughout (symlink rejection, traversal validation, atomic directory replacement); single large run_skills_sync orchestrator over many focused helpers.

nodes.name: ResolvedSkill
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing a fully resolved skill entry: its name, source name and type, subpath within the source, and deployment mode (symlink vs copy).

nodes.name: parse_duration
nodes.summary: Parses a human duration string (e.g. '7d', '12h') into seconds via a regex, raising ValueError on malformed input.

nodes.name: load_skills_manifest
nodes.summary: Reads and validates the skills manifest JSON file, ensuring it is a mapping and defaulting the sources and skills sections.

nodes.name: _safe_target
nodes.summary: Validates a skill name and resolves its deployment path under the target root, rejecting any path that escapes the root to prevent traversal.

nodes.name: resolve_skills
nodes.summary: Walks the manifest's skills section, validating each entry's source reference, subpath, and mode, and produces a sorted list of ResolvedSkill objects.

nodes.name: load_state
nodes.summary: Loads the persisted skill-sync state JSON, tolerating a missing or corrupt file by returning a fresh default structure.

nodes.name: write_state
nodes.summary: Serializes the skill-sync state to JSON, creating the parent directory as needed.

nodes.name: ensure_git_source
nodes.summary: Clones or fetches a git-backed skill source into the cache, honoring a TTL and pinned ref, and updates the state record with the resolved commit.

nodes.name: _assert_tree_has_no_symlinks
nodes.summary: Recursively scans a directory tree and raises if any symlink is present, guarding against unsafe skill payloads before copy deployment.

nodes.name: _replace_directory_from_copy
nodes.summary: Atomically replaces a target directory with a fresh copytree using a temp staging dir and rename-with-backup, restoring the backup on failure.

nodes.name: deploy_skill
nodes.summary: Deploys a resolved skill to its target either as a symlink or as a symlink-free copy with a managed marker, depending on the requested mode.

nodes.name: garbage_collect
nodes.summary: Removes deployments from a previous sync that are no longer in the current skill set, carefully distinguishing managed symlinks and copies from user-owned paths.

nodes.name: run_skills_sync
nodes.summary: Top-level orchestrator for skill sync: loads and merges the manifest with the machine overlay, resolves skills, refreshes git caches, deploys each skill, garbage-collects stale ones, and writes back the new state.

nodes.name: skills_cli.py
nodes.summary: Argparse CLI for the skill-sync subsystem, exposing the `sync-skills` console script that parses manifest/home/repo-root options and calls run_skills_sync.

nodes.name: build_parser
nodes.summary: Builds the argparse parser for the skill-sync CLI with manifest, machine-config, home, and repo-root path options.

nodes.name: cli
nodes.summary: Entry function that parses argv, expands user paths, and invokes run_skills_sync for the skill-sync command.

nodes.name: sync.py
nodes.summary: The heart of the MCP sync tool: reads the master MCP config, merges machine overlays and per-tool overrides, and fans out tool-specific configs (codex TOML, Claude Code, Copilot, opencode, and other generic MCP formats) to their target locations.
nodes.languageNotes: Format-specific transform_to_*_format functions plus a declarative SyncTarget dataclass driving a build/sync pipeline; no runtime dependencies, uses stdlib string.Template for variable substitution.

nodes.name: SyncTarget
nodes.summary: Dataclass describing one fan-out target (destination, transform function, template/override keys, legacy paths) with build() and sync() methods that produce and write the final config.

nodes.name: load_master_config
nodes.summary: Loads and parses the master MCP config JSON from the given path.

nodes.name: _is_server_enabled
nodes.summary: Interprets a server config's `enabled` flag across its many truthy/falsey forms (bool, string, int) to decide whether the server should be emitted.

nodes.name: _filter_enabled_servers
nodes.summary: Returns only the servers whose enabled flag evaluates truthy, dropping disabled entries from the output map.

nodes.name: deep_merge
nodes.summary: Recursively merges an override mapping into a base mapping, with special handling for list-append keys and nested dicts so overlays compose predictably.

nodes.name: sync_codex_mcp
nodes.summary: Builds the Codex config by appending a rendered TOML MCP-servers section onto the base template and writing it to the codex config path.

nodes.name: _render_codex_mcp_section
nodes.summary: Renders the per-server TOML lines for Codex (command, args, env) with proper TOML string escaping.

nodes.name: sync_copilot_cli_config
nodes.summary: Patches the GitHub Copilot CLI config in place, preserving existing auth tokens from a backup while updating the MCP server entries.

nodes.name: patch_claude_code_config
nodes.summary: Merges the enabled MCP servers into the existing Claude Code config JSON without clobbering unrelated user settings, then writes it back.

nodes.name: transform_to_copilot_format
nodes.summary: Transforms the master server map into the Copilot-specific MCP config shape, filtering disabled servers and stripping internal fields.

nodes.name: transform_to_opencode_format
nodes.summary: Transforms the master server map into opencode's MCP schema, mapping command/args/env into opencode's local-server representation.

nodes.name: _build_targets
nodes.summary: Constructs the declarative list of SyncTarget objects (one per downstream tool) that drive the JSON-based fan-out portion of the sync.

nodes.name: run_sync
nodes.summary: Top-level sync orchestrator: loads the master config, merges the machine overlay, runs every JSON SyncTarget, and invokes the bespoke codex/Claude Code/Copilot syncs.

nodes.name: codex.base.toml
nodes.summary: Base Codex TOML template onto which the sync tool appends the generated MCP-servers section; carries Codex feature defaults.

nodes.name: opencode.base.json
nodes.summary: Base opencode JSON template providing provider and schema defaults, merged with the generated MCP server block during sync.

nodes.summary: Package marker for token_auditor that declares the package version string.

nodes.name: core/__init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the token_auditor.core subpackage holding the pure parsing and pricing logic.

nodes.name: claude.py
nodes.summary: Pure parsing pipeline for Claude session JSONL: extracts per-message token snapshots, dedupes them, aggregates usage, resolves model pricing, computes costs, and finalizes a session audit.
nodes.languageNotes: Stateless functions chained by parse_claude_events; uses dict-based message dedup keyed by message id.

nodes.name: codex.py
nodes.summary: Pure parsing pipeline for Codex session JSONL: extracts per-event deltas (model, reasoning effort, cumulative usage), folds them into a running state, then resolves pricing and finalizes the audit.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses functools.reduce over a CodexState accumulator; token usage is cumulative-total rather than per-message.

nodes.name: constants.py
nodes.summary: Shared constants for the token_auditor core: project name, session-file glob patterns, the OpenCode DB default path, and the per-million-token USD pricing table for Codex and Claude models.
nodes.languageNotes: Pricing is a nested dict[str, dict[str, dict[str, float]]] keyed by provider, model, and token category.

nodes.name: jsonl.py
nodes.summary: Pure JSONL decoding utility that parses session lines into dict events, raising SessionParseError with file/line context on malformed JSON.

nodes.name: TestFilterEnabledServers
nodes.summary: Tests _filter_enabled_servers across enabled/disabled/missing-field states, config preservation, empty/non-dict inputs, and enabled-over-disabled precedence.

nodes.name: TestEnablementFieldStripping
nodes.summary: Verifies each output transform strips the internal disabled/enabled field from the emitted per-tool config.

nodes.name: TestTransformWithEnabledFlag
nodes.summary: Checks each transform filters out disabled servers and removes the enabled marker, including opencode's own enabled:true output convention.

nodes.name: TestEnabledFlagWithMachineOverlay
nodes.summary: Tests that a machine overlay can disable a master server, re-enable a disabled one, and add new enabled servers via deep_merge.

nodes.name: TestEnabledFlagIntegration
nodes.summary: Runs run_sync end-to-end asserting disabled servers are excluded from outputs and that overlay and tool-override layers can disable servers, with backward compatibility for servers lacking the flag.

nodes.name: TestCodexSyncWithEnabledFlag
nodes.summary: Verifies sync_codex_mcp omits disabled servers from the generated codex config.toml.

nodes.name: TestClaudeCodePatchWithEnabledFlag
nodes.summary: Tests patch_claude_code_config filters disabled servers, strips the enabled field, removes previously-synced now-disabled servers, and honors tool-override disabling.

nodes.name: TestLoadMachineConfig
nodes.summary: Tests load_machine_config returns an empty dict for None path, missing file, or invalid JSON, and the parsed object for a valid file.

nodes.name: TestMachineOverlayMerge
nodes.summary: Tests deep_merge overlay behavior: adding new servers, overriding existing ones, adding top-level keys, and treating an empty overlay as identity.

nodes.name: TestRunSyncWithMachineConfig
nodes.summary: End-to-end run_sync tests confirming machine-overlay servers, disabled flags, and top-level keys propagate correctly (or are absent) across the per-tool output files.

nodes.name: configure
nodes.summary: Configures root logging level and format for the token_auditor CLI.

nodes.name: extract_claude_message_snapshot
nodes.summary: Builds a ClaudeMessageSnapshot (model, message id, token usage) from a single Claude session event, or None when usage is absent.

nodes.name: aggregate_claude_usage
nodes.summary: Sums input, output, cache-creation, and cache-read tokens across deduped message snapshots into a single TokenUsage.

nodes.name: compute_claude_costs
nodes.summary: Resolves per-message pricing models and calculates per-message and total USD costs for a deduped set of Claude snapshots.

nodes.name: finalize_claude_audit
nodes.summary: Assembles the final Claude session audit object from aggregated usage, model metadata, computed costs, and the latest timestamp.

nodes.name: parse_claude_events
nodes.summary: Top-level entry that walks Claude session events, extracts and dedupes message snapshots, and returns a finalized audit.

nodes.name: extract_codex_event_delta
nodes.summary: Derives a CodexDelta (session metadata, model, reasoning effort, or cumulative token usage) from a single Codex session event.

nodes.name: finalize_codex_state
nodes.summary: Converts the accumulated CodexState into a finalized audit, ensuring total tokens, resolving pricing, and calculating costs.

nodes.name: parse_codex_events
nodes.summary: Top-level entry that reduces Codex session event deltas into a CodexState and returns a finalized audit.

nodes.name: decode_jsonl_lines
nodes.summary: Decodes JSONL session lines into dict events, raising SessionParseError with file and line context on malformed JSON.

nodes.name: opencode.py
nodes.summary: Parses Opencode session usage rows from the sqlite store, normalizes/matches cwd against row roots to pick the right session, and aggregates per-session token counts and costs into an audit dict.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a frozen dataclass (OpencodeUsageRow) plus typing.cast helpers to safely coerce loosely-typed sqlite JSON payloads.

nodes.name: pricing.py
nodes.summary: Resolves a provider/model string to a canonical pricing key via direct, alias, and prefix lookups, then computes per-category USD costs from token counts using the per-1M-token rate tables.

nodes.name: render.py
nodes.summary: Renders an audit result as either ANSI-colored aligned text tables (summary/token/cost sections) or sorted-key JSON, with color-enablement precedence logic.

nodes.name: session_resolution.py
nodes.summary: Computes the Claude project slug/directory for a working directory and selects the most-recently-modified session file, preferring the current project over the global fallback.

nodes.name: types.py
nodes.summary: Defines the shared frozen dataclasses (TokenUsage, CodexDelta/State, ClaudeMessageSnapshot) and the SessionParseError exception used across the token-auditor core.
nodes.languageNotes: All models are immutable frozen dataclasses, reinforcing the pure-functional core design.

nodes.name: utils.py
nodes.summary: Tiny coercion helpers (safe_int, safe_float) that convert loosely-typed values to numbers, returning zero on malformed input.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for the codax/token-auditor tool: builds the argparse parser, resolves the latest Codex/Claude/Opencode session file, dispatches to the provider parser, and prints the audit as text or JSON.
nodes.languageNotes: Acts as the composition root, injecting shell/io_adapters functions into the pure core parsers.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the token_auditor.shell subpackage that houses impure I/O adapters.

nodes.name: io_adapters.py
nodes.summary: Thin impure adapters wrapping filesystem, environment, and TTY access (read_lines, glob_paths, path_exists/mtime, env_value, is_tty) so the core stays side-effect free.
nodes.languageNotes: Isolates all OS/filesystem side effects in one module, enabling easy monkeypatching in tests.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker making the tests directory an importable package.

nodes.name: test_cli_integration.py
nodes.summary: End-to-end CLI integration tests exercising main() across Codex/Claude/Opencode session discovery, text/JSON output, color modes, failure paths, and the python -m entry point.

nodes.name: test_codex_core.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the Codex event parser covering delta extraction, reasoning-effort fallbacks, state reduction, last-token-count selection, total recomputation, and zero-cost handling for unknown models.

nodes.name: test_jsonl.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the JSONL decoder verifying valid decoding, coercion of non-mapping records to empty events, and line-aware parse errors.

nodes.name: test_logging.py
nodes.summary: Unit test asserting the logging configure() helper calls logging.basicConfig with the expected level and format parameters.

nodes.name: test_opencode_core.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for Opencode row parsing: latest-matching-cwd selection, subdirectory matching, fallback to latest session, none-results for missing usage, and path-prefix edge cases.

nodes.summary: Unit tests for pricing resolution and cost calculation across the current model fleet (gpt-5.5, gpt-5.4 family, Claude, Codex), alias/prefix rules, unknown-model zeroing, and long-context billing.

nodes.name: test_render.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the renderer: color-enablement precedence, ANSI painting, USD/token formatting, row formatters, long-context premium row inclusion, and sorted-key JSON output.

nodes.name: test_utils.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests asserting safe_int and safe_float return zero for malformed inputs.

nodes.name: token_auditor.iml
nodes.summary: IntelliJ/PyCharm module descriptor marking src/ as the source root, excluding .venv, and binding the uv-managed Python SDK for the token_auditor project.

nodes.name: OpencodeUsageRow
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding a single parsed Opencode usage row: session/model/path metadata plus per-category token counts and cost.

nodes.name: extract_opencode_usage_row
nodes.summary: Builds an OpencodeUsageRow from a raw sqlite/JSON row, safely coercing nested token, cache, time, and path fields into typed values.

nodes.name: choose_opencode_session_id
nodes.summary: Selects the session id to audit, preferring rows whose root/cwd match the current working directory, otherwise the latest by creation time.

nodes.name: parse_opencode_rows
nodes.summary: Aggregates extracted Opencode rows for the chosen session into a final audit dict, summing token categories and costs and recomputing missing totals.

nodes.name: resolve_pricing_model
nodes.summary: Maps a provider/model string to a canonical pricing key using direct table hits, exact aliases, and longest-prefix alias matching.

nodes.name: calculate_costs
nodes.summary: Computes a per-category USD cost breakdown from token counts and a resolved pricing model, handling cached/cache-creation token subtraction and long-context billing.

nodes.name: zero_costs
nodes.summary: Returns a fully zeroed cost breakdown dict used when a model has no known pricing.

nodes.name: decide_color_enabled
nodes.summary: Resolves whether ANSI color should be emitted by applying color-mode, NO_COLOR, TTY, and terminal-type precedence rules.

nodes.name: format_cost_rows
nodes.summary: Builds the cost-section table rows from an audit, formatting USD values and conditionally appending provider-billed and long-context-premium lines.

nodes.name: render_text_audit
nodes.summary: Renders a full audit as colored, aligned text with header, summary, token, and cost sections joined into a single string.

nodes.name: format_summary_rows
nodes.summary: Extracts the session-summary label/value rows (session id, model, timestamps, totals) from an audit dict.

nodes.name: format_token_rows
nodes.summary: Builds the token-count table rows from an audit, formatting each category with human-friendly thousands separators.

nodes.name: choose_claude_session_path
nodes.summary: Picks the Claude session file to audit, preferring the latest within the current project's directory and falling back to the latest global session.

nodes.name: TokenUsage
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass capturing per-category token counts (input, cached, cache-creation, output, reasoning, total) shared across providers.

nodes.name: CodexDelta
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass representing an incremental Codex usage delta with session/model/effort/timestamp metadata and a TokenUsage payload.

nodes.name: CodexState
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding the accumulated Codex session state reduced from successive deltas.

nodes.name: ClaudeMessageSnapshot
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass capturing a single Claude assistant message's id, model, timestamp, and token usage for deduplication.

nodes.name: build_parser
nodes.summary: Constructs the argparse ArgumentParser defining all token-auditor CLI flags (provider homes, cwd, color mode, JSON output, verbosity).

nodes.name: parse_opencode_session_usage
nodes.summary: Opens the Opencode sqlite database, queries assistant usage rows, decodes their JSON payloads, and delegates to the core parser to produce an audit.
nodes.languageNotes: Directly uses sqlite3 and wraps decode failures in SessionParseError.

nodes.name: _resolve_session_file
nodes.summary: Resolves which session file/database to audit based on CLI args, expanding paths and falling back to latest-session discovery per provider.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Top-level CLI driver: parses args, configures logging, resolves and parses the session, then prints the audit as text or JSON, returning an exit code.

nodes.summary: Finds the most recent Claude session file by globbing the project-specific and global session directories and choosing the latest.

nodes.name: parse_codex_session_usage

nodes.name: parse_claude_session_usage
nodes.summary: Reads a Claude JSONL session file via the IO adapter, decodes its lines, and delegates to the core Claude event parser.

nodes.name: read_lines
nodes.summary: Opens a file and returns its lines as a tuple, isolating filesystem reads from the pure core.

nodes.name: write_session_file
nodes.summary: Test helper that creates parent directories and writes a list of records as JSONL to a session file path.

nodes.name: write_opencode_db
nodes.summary: Test helper that builds a sqlite Opencode database fixture, inserting message rows with JSON-encoded usage payloads for integration tests.

nodes.summary: Pure helper that coerces a loosely-typed value to an int, returning 0 on TypeError/ValueError.

layers.name: Shell Environment

layers.description: Dev-container build context (per-stack Dockerfiles, docker-compose profiles, entrypoint), GitHub Actions lint/test pipelines, the macOS LaunchAgent for the Xcode MCP proxy, and IntelliJ run configurations.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Repo-level guides and agent instructions: README, CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md, the docs/ tree (cheatsheets, networking, AI-tool setup) and the superpowers plans/specs.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to learn what this repo is: a personal macOS dotfiles repository managed by chezmoi, with age-encrypted secrets and three vendored Python tools. The CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md pair (a symlinked twin) is the agent-facing companion that documents the per-tool commands, chezmoi naming conventions, and the machine-capability gating model that the rest of this tour unpacks. Read these first to get the map before diving into the territory.

tour.title: Bootstrap & The Gating Model
tour.description: Every chezmoi run begins with .chezmoi.toml.tmpl, which prompts once per machine for a machine-type and email, then writes the .machine and .email data variables that drive all downstream decisions. Those decisions are resolved against machines.toml -- the single source of truth that maps each machine (personal-mac, work-mac, lab-mac) to capability booleans like tiling, mcp, skills, dev, and infra. This table is the heart of the repo: it is the most-depended-upon config in the project, and almost every later step gates its behavior on a lookup into it.
tour.languageLesson: Chezmoi source files are Go text/template (.tmpl) files. Capability gates read `(index .machines .machine).<capability>` -- `index` does a map lookup keyed by the active machine name, so adding a new machine is one new row in the table rather than edits at every gate site.

tour.title: Gating & Encryption In Practice
tour.description: Building on the capability table from Step 2, .chezmoiignore is the templated manifest that decides which source files actually get deployed to a given machine -- it combines static excludes (the in-repo Python tools) with capability conditionals so a headless lab box skips the window manager while a work box skips personal secrets. .chezmoiencrypt.toml declares the age cipher and the glob patterns (env files, token/secret files) that chezmoi keeps encrypted at rest. Together these two files implement the 'right files, decrypted only where allowed' guarantee the README promises.
tour.languageLesson: chezmoi encrypts with age (a modern, simple alternative to GPG). Source files named `encrypted_*.age` are ciphertext at rest in the repo and are transparently decrypted on `chezmoi apply` using an identity sourced from 1Password.

tour.title: MCP Fan-Out: Source of Truth
tour.description: Now we reach the repo's signature subsystem. The dotfiles drive many AI dev tools (Claude, Codex, OpenCode, Copilot) that each want their MCP server list in a different file format. Rather than maintain those by hand, mcp-master.json holds the shared base, and the per-machine overlays layer machine-specific servers on top -- the personal overlay adds the local hippo brain, work adds the AWS CCAPI server, lab adds only a read-only Grafana endpoint. This 'base + overlay' shape mirrors the gating model from Step 2 and feeds the engine in the next step.

tour.title: MCP Fan-Out: The Engine
tour.description: This is the core of the project. mcp_sync is a vendored uv tool whose cli.py parses arguments and whose sync.py -- the single most-depended-upon module in the entire codebase -- merges the master config from Step 4 with the active machine overlay and transforms the result into each tool's native format (Copilot JSON, Codex TOML, OpenCode, generic mcpServers). The run_after_sync-mcp.sh.tmpl post-apply hook is what ties it back into chezmoi: after every apply it auto-detects the deployed overlay and invokes the tool, and it self-gates on the mcp capability so it no-ops on machines that don't run AI tooling. The README and dedicated docs walk through the same flow in prose.
tour.languageLesson: Python's `python -m mcp_sync` entry works because `__main__.py` delegates to `cli.py`; the `pyproject.toml` `[project.scripts]` table also exposes `sync-mcp-configs` as a console command. The transform functions are pure functions (master+overlay in, formatted dict out), which is what makes the merge logic so easy to unit-test.

tour.title: Skills Fan-Out (Parallel Path)
tour.description: The same mcp_sync project ships a second, parallel fan-out for Claude skills. skills.py and skills_cli.py read skills-master.json -- which lists skill sources (a pinned upstream git repo plus a local personal source) -- and deploy them into ~/.claude/skills via copy or symlink, with garbage collection of stale ones. The run_after_sync-skills.sh.tmpl hook mirrors the MCP hook from Step 5, self-gating on the skills capability. Studying this alongside the MCP path shows how one engine generalizes a 'master + machine overlay + post-apply hook' pattern across two different concerns.

tour.title: Python Tool: aws_config_gen
tour.description: The second vendored tool generates AWS SSO profiles. This tightly-coupled cluster shows a clean layered design: cli.py orchestrates, discovery.py walks the SSO account/role catalog, naming.py applies the profile-naming rules, config_writer.py merges the result into ~/.aws/config, and types.py defines the shared dataclasses every other module depends on. Like the MCP sync, it is wired into chezmoi by a capability-gated post-apply hook (run_after_sync-aws-config) that only runs on machines with the aws_sso capability -- so it stays dormant on personal and lab machines.

tour.title: Python Tool: token_auditor (codax)
tour.description: The third tool, exposed as the codax CLI, audits LLM token usage across Claude, Codex, and OpenCode sessions. Its cluster centers on main.py, which builds the argument parser, resolves the latest session file, dispatches to the right provider parser (claude.py here), and renders the audit. types.py and pricing.py are the shared foundation -- pricing.py holds the per-model rate card that turns raw token counts into dollar figures. This tool has the highest fan-out in the repo because main.py wires together every provider and rendering module.

tour.title: Editor, Terminal & Window Manager
tour.description: With the tooling understood, we move to the desktop the dotfiles configure. Neovim is a LazyVim setup: init.lua bootstraps, lazy.lua wires the plugin manager (the most-depended-upon Lua file), and plugins/lsp.lua layers language-server config on top. AeroSpace is the tiling window manager whose .tmpl config carries per-machine workspace assignments and is itself gated by the tiling capability, SketchyBar renders the status bar, and tmux.conf plus its claude-pane-monitor.sh script paint per-window Claude session state into the status line with stoplight colors.
tour.languageLesson: Neovim/LazyVim config is Lua: `lazy.lua` calls `require('lazy').setup(...)` with a spec table, and each file under `lua/plugins/` returns a plugin-spec table that LazyVim merges. The whole config is declarative data describing what to install and how to configure it.

tour.title: Shell Environment & Secrets
tour.description: The shell startup chain begins at ~/.zshenv (dot_zshenv), which pins the XDG directories and ZDOTDIR, then defers to the real config under dot_config/zsh. dot_zshrc is the interactive entry point and has high fan-out because it sources a profile.d/ chain of modular fragments -- machine-gated shell-function and secrets files like personal-secrets.zsh. Actual API keys live in age-encrypted env files (encrypted_dot_env.age) that decrypt on apply, closing the loop on the encryption rules you saw in Step 3.

tour.title: AI Assistant Configuration
tour.description: Beyond the MCP/skills fan-out, the repo carries direct settings for each AI assistant. modify_settings.json.tmpl is a chezmoi modify-script that idempotently merges managed Claude Code settings (env vars, hooks, status line, enabled plugins) onto the live ~/.claude/settings.json, gating the hippo SessionStart hook and dev/work-only plugins by capability. dot_claude/CLAUDE.md is the global, machine-wide agent instruction file (distinct from the repo's own CLAUDE.md in Step 1), and dot_codex/config.toml pins the Codex model and feature flags.
tour.languageLesson: A chezmoi `modify_` script receives the current target file on stdin and writes the desired version to stdout, so it can merge into a file the user/app also edits rather than overwriting it -- the right pattern for app settings.json files that get mutated at runtime.

tour.title: Provisioning, Containers & CI/CD
tour.description: This step covers how the machine and the code get provisioned and verified. Brewfile.tmpl declares Homebrew packages with capability-gated blocks, so the WM tools install only where tiling is enabled and dev/infra tooling only on machines that develop. The dev-container profiles ship per-stack Dockerfiles (the python-uv image shown here) for reproducible project environments. The Justfile is the local task runner, while the GitHub Actions workflows lint and test each Python tool (mcp-sync-ci) plus run repo-wide hygiene checks (dotfiles-hygiene-ci) -- the same checks pre-commit enforces locally.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows use `on` triggers scoped with `paths:` so each tool's CI only runs when its directory changes, `jobs` run in parallel, and `steps` run sequentially. Pinning `uv` and caching its download dir keeps Python installs fast across runs.

tour.title: Verification Skills (Capstone)
tour.description: Finish where the repo's safety net lives: in-repo Claude skills that verify the very systems this tour explained. machine-capability-audit (driven by audit.sh) statically checks the gating model from Step 2 for orphan capabilities and prefix-gates that should migrate to the table. mcp-sync-verify lints, tests, and sandbox-diffs the MCP fan-out from Step 5 before you trust an apply. chezmoi-verify renders every .tmpl through execute-template to catch parse errors that a plain `chezmoi diff` would silently hide. Knowing these exist tells a newcomer how to change this repo safely.
